other_observers: Brandon Percival, Bryan Patrick, Jeannie and Ron Mitchell, Kelly Shipe and her brother, Aaron.; Dan and John Maynard first found the bird in the A.M. of the same day.
duration: first 3 seconds and later for about 2 minutes
specific_location: Fountain Creek Regional Park, adjacent to the nature trail by the defuct sewer pond, very close to where a Black Phoebe was located a few years ago and close to a trail monuent marking the mileage, but no I didn't write it down, and also where a Winter Wren (and maybe a PAWR) was also found. PAWR at same spot last December to January of this year.
description: This was a bright yellow (ventrally) bird with a warbler-like bill.   The feature that stood out most was the bright yellow chin, throat, and breast that constasted strongly with a whitish lower belly and very olive-green crown, face and back. It had gray-olive wings with two strong whitish wingbars. I forgot to look at leg color. On the face, there was a yellow eyering the also extended to form a yellow spectacle, very noticeable.Behaviors: First seen briefly perched high in a tree in the sun, then flew off to a distant tree and then flew almost immediately out of sight. It then reappered low in a Siberian elm where it preened for a few seconds but mostly sat with feathers fluff out and facing the sunlight.Call: none heardPlumage: adult winter
